title: U.S. War Against Iran Enters a New Phase
summary: As President Trump resumes his war, the focus is now on the Strait of Hormuz. But it remains unclear how far the U.S. military will go to exert control.

title: For Israel, the U.S.-Iran Hostilities Have Created an Uneasy Limbo
summary: Officials see a return to full-blown war as preferable to an agreement that fails to curb the threats Iran poses to Israel. Meanwhile, they wait.

title: Trump Flip-Flops on Strait of Hormuz Plan, and U.S. Cyclospora Outbreak Spreads
summary: Plus, Chipotle is coming to Mexico.

title: China’s Economy Grows at Slowest Pace in Years
summary: Economic growth of 4.3 percent in the second quarter, versus the same period last year, reflected a broad slump outside of the country’s export-oriented manufacturing might.

title: China’s Chip Champion to Raise Billions in Race for A.I. Control
summary: CXMT, a linchpin in China’s drive to develop homegrown artificial intelligence technology, is aiming to raise nearly $10 billion in a blockbuster public offering in Shanghai.

title: U.S. Presses China to Free American Seismologist Accused of Spying
summary: President Trump has urged Xi Jinping to release the scientist, Youlin Chen, the man’s family says. Dr. Chen studied the detection of North Korean nuclear tests.

title: Colombian Immigrant Killed by ICE in Maine Had Legal Status, Father Says
summary: The father of Joan Sebastian Guerrero, the man fatally shot by a federal immigration agent on Monday, said his son had been working two jobs to support his wife and daughter.

title: Videos Detail Moments Surrounding Fatal ICE Shooting in Biddeford, Maine
summary: Footage obtained by The New York Times shows ICE agents in the moments before and after the killing of Joan Sebastian Guerrero. The circumstances of the shooting remain unclear.

title: An ICE Shooting in Maine Puts Pressure on Senator Susan Collins
summary: The Democrats hoping to challenge Ms. Collins, a vulnerable Republican, have seized on the fatal incident, drawing attention to her record on immigration matters.

title: ICE Shootings Put Spotlight on Lack of Body Cameras
summary: Kristi Noem, the former homeland security secretary, said five months ago that the Trump administration would “rapidly” deploy body cameras to immigration agents across the country. About half still don’t have them.

title: Ukraine Pounds Russian Ships in Its Campaign to Cut Off Crimea
summary: After striking roads and railways, Kyiv is now focusing on sea routes as it tries to disrupt fuel supplies and pressure Russia to end the war.

title: Cyclospora Cases Rise Rapidly, With No Source Yet Confirmed
summary: The parasite, which has sickened thousands across the U.S., is notoriously difficult to track. Funding cuts haven’t made the job easier.

title: How to Stay Safe From Cyclospora, a Parasite Causing Severe Intestinal Illness
summary: Cyclospora, a parasite that causes intestinal illness, is sickening many people across the United States. Health experts have tips for avoiding infection.

title: As Confirmation Hearing Nears, Todd Blanche’s Role in Epstein Case Draws Scrutiny
summary: Todd Blanche’s role in interviewing Ghislaine Maxwell, a longtime associate of the convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein, will be front and center as he seeks to become attorney general.

title: Should Epstein’s Friends Be Punished? An Elite Harvard Club Is Torn.
summary: At Harvard, students, alumni and professors debate whether a storied campus group should cut ties with one of Jeffrey Epstein’s closest associates, and whether Harvard itself should do the same.

title: He Has a $25 Million Bounty on His Head but Is Also a U.S. Partner in Venezuela
summary: A list of alleged crimes has not prevented Diosdado Cabello, and other Venezuelan officials, from working with a U.S. government that once hounded them.

title: Senator Mark Kelly Amasses Nearly $25 Million Campaign War Chest
summary: The Arizona Democrat said he had raised and given away $10 million to fellow members of his party and committees as he mulls a presidential run in 2028.

title: Platner Was Toxic. But Democrats Could Learn From His Politics.
summary: His progressive, populist message was able to occupy a kind of middle ground in the party’s primary electorate.

title: Paramedics Help Save Lives in New York. Can They Afford to Live There?
summary: Some of New York City’s emergency medical workers make just $18 an hour. Their boss, Zohran Mamdani, has staked his mayoralty on making New York City more affordable for all.

title: Mamdani Will Advance Bronx Bus Lane Project to Improve Transit Speeds
summary: The road redesign to prioritize buses, the first such project in the borough, had been put on hold after the Trump administration objected to a similar plan in Manhattan.

title: The American E.V. Has Been Crushed. Will It Take the U.S. Auto Industry With It?
summary: The largest U.S. automakers have backed away from electric vehicles, even as global sales are booming. The decision may make them obsolete.

title: We’re About to Find Out Whether Republican Senators Can Still Say No
summary: Republicans should have the decency, patriotism and self-respect to vote against Mr. Blanche.

title: ‘It’s Chaos’: What New York’s Suburbs Can Tell Us About the Mood of the Country
summary: The midterms are looming. We asked people in New York City’s suburbs how they feel about the state of America.

title: Fatal Encounters
summary: We look at shootings by federal immigration agents.

title: Late Night Calls Lindsey Graham’s Senate Seat ‘an Inheritance Jackpot’
summary: “A Senate seat? Most people just get a garage full of damp boxes,” Michael Kosta said of Graham’s sister filling the late senator’s seat on “The Daily Show.”

title: Biden Will Release Memoir Just After the November Midterms
summary: The former president announced that the book, “Promise Me, America,” will come out on Nov. 17, and will cover the tumultuous events of his presidency as well as his re-election bid.
